title: Accelerating Quantum Computing Research Using AI and Robotics
description: Chapter 2 of dossier QC-Accelerate-002. Robots have run in labs for forty years; the news is the closed loop — AI chooses the experiment, machines run it, results steer the next one, around the clock. This chapter maps where that loop already turns in quantum computing (real-time neural surface-code decoding, reinforcement-learned gates and error-correcting codes), where it is only half-built (cryogenic wafer probers, autonomous chemistry, and the A-Lab's cautionary correction), and what would close it on qubits themselves — four ranked bottlenecks with dated, falsifiable horizons: fabrication-in-the-loop, the learning signal, cryogenic tempo, and orchestration. A race section reads the allied bloc against China on rates rather than stocks — the PPP R&D crossover, the top-cited-scientist flip, and quantum-hardware trajectories — and splits its verdict where the evidence splits. It closes on the doors iteration cannot open: the conceptual gates, where an existence question stays open in the literature. Every claim carries an honest label. An Open Dossier publication: executable claims under CI, published adversarial review, and OpenTimestamps-anchored releases.
